description: Add detailed email logging and SMTP diagnostics to debug email delivery failures in production. Remove silent exception swallowing in exam submission, add SMTP diagnostic logging at startup, and add a test-email endpoint.
steps: In app/routers/exam.py, replace the silent 'except Exception: pass' around send_result_email with detailed logging that shows whether the email was sent and logs any error with type and message.
In app/services/email_service.py, add a diagnostic print before SMTP connection attempt showing the target email, SMTP host, port, and user.
In app/services/email_service.py, ensure the except block includes type(e).__name__ in error message.
In main.py, add SMTP configured diagnostic after existing GROQ diagnostics.
In app/routers/api.py, add a GET /api/test-email endpoint that accepts a 'to' query parameter and sends a test email to verify SMTP configuration.
acceptance_criteria: Email sending errors in exam submission are logged with descriptive messages instead of being silently swallowed
SMTP connection attempts are logged with host/port/user details before attempting to connect
Startup diagnostics show whether SMTP is configured
A /api/test-email?to=email endpoint exists to verify SMTP configuration
All existing tests continue to pass
findings: All 37 tests pass. The test-email endpoint correctly returns SMTP_USER/SMTP_PASSWORD not configured errors when env vars are missing. Startup diagnostics now show SMTP status. The send_result_email in exam.py now logs success/failure instead of silently swallowing exceptions.
